title: SACRAMENTO — The Golden State Innovation Act has garnered a broad coalition of support since its introduction on February 11, 2021. Patient-advocacy groups, life sciences companies
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Restoring the Research and Development (R&D) tax credit will provide California with the tools to save lives.",
    "Tax incentives are critical policy levers for the development of life-saving vaccines, treatments, and therapies.",
    "California is one of the largest biopharmaceutical innovation hubs in the world.",
    "The success of California's life sciences ecosystem is built on supportive practices that help medical technology companies grow and thrive.",
    "In 2019, California companies had 1,380 new medicines in the pipeline, including 455 for cancer and 136 for central nervous system disorders.",
    "California's Research and Development tax credit incentivizes biotechnology companies to invest in treatments and vaccines for COVID-19.",
    "California's life sciences industry is an economic driver in the Bay Area and generates well-paying jobs.",
    "The rapid development of COVID vaccines exemplifies the benefits of biotech research for health and the economy.",
    "Investing in medical research generates new sales revenues and taxes for California and creates new jobs.",
    "California's ecosystem for life sciences yields well-trained PhDs and attracts venture capitalists to invest in young companies.",
    "The life sciences sector is experiencing growth due to strong spending towards innovative technologies and platforms such as gene and cell therapies.",
    "Tax credits help biotechnology start-ups make discoveries that lead to medicines protecting and improving health.",
    "Incentivizing research supports California's ability to lead in the life sciences field."
  ]
}
```
